details.text: this is the first of a number of surahs that begin with the huroof al-muqatta’aat حم (haa' meem).
Derivative: مَقْتُ means to have a strong hatred and revulsion. Can include the meanings of disgust and disappointment.

Derivative: الرُّوحَ – الرُّوحَ can have multiple meanings and in this verse various meanings have been provided in accordance with its various possible meanings. These meanings include Jibreel (as) and the human soul that is removed every night and returned to whomever Allah wills. However, the majority view is that الرُّوحَ in this context refers to the وَحْي (Wahy -divine revelation) that Allah sends to whomever He wills from His slaves i.e., Prophets. The meaning of Jibreel (as) and وَحْي (Wahy -divine revelation) can be combined in this understanding.

Derivative: التَّلَاقِ – التَّلَاقِ means the meeting or coming together of more than one party. Consequently, يَوْمَ التَّلَاقِ refers to the Day of Judgement, the Day in which the inhabitants of the earth will meat the inhabitants of the heavens, the creation with their Creator, and the creation with other creation, the first generations with later generations, the worshippers and those who were worshipped, the oppressed and oppressors etc i.e., the day when all will be brought together.

Derivative: الْقَهَّارِ – The One who cannot be denied, the Indomitable. It is the Mubaalaghah (exaggerated) form of القاهر. It is linked to power and capability that always has its way and cannot be surmounted.
Derivative: الْآزِفَةِ – Refers to a time that is approaching and getting closer and closer.
Derivative: كَاظِمِينَ from كَظَمَ which means to suppress, to put down. Here, in reference to the hearts reaching the throats, it is like they are swallowing and suppressing their hearts.
Derivative: حَمِيمٍ – It is derived from حَمَّ which means to become hot or to have a fever. From that a close friend is derived, like a warm friendship.

- خَائِنَةَ الْأَعْيُنِ – Literally “The betrayal of the eyes” and refers to the stolen or furtive glances. Those prohibited glances which others are unaware of, but Allah has captured and is fully aware of.
Derivative: الْعَزِيزِ – One interpretation is that this refers to the attribute of being the ultimate authority.
Derivative: وَاقٍ from وَقَى – يَقِي – وِقَايَة which means to protect. وَاقٍ is a protector, someone to shield someone or something.

- ضَلَالٍ from ضَلَّ can mean misguidance and error but can also mean to become lost and lose one’s way. However, in this context فِي ضَلَالٍ implies that it will be lost and go to waste i.e., avail and achieve nothing. For example, ضَلَّ سَعْيُهُ is a common expression to mean that “His efforts were in vain” i.e., came to bear no benefit or fruits.

Derivative: ذَرُو is from وَذَرَ – يَذِرُ. It is only used in the مُضارِع (imperfect) form and is most commonly used in the أَمْر (command) form: ذَرْ. It means to let or leave in general or more specifically like: Leave me alone to do something or let me do something. It can also be used to mean “to leave behind”.

Derivative: التَّوْبِ (Tawb) – It refers to the weakest form of repentance from the root verb of تَابَ. There is تَوْبَة (Tawbah) which is stronger and then there is مَتابٌ (Mataab) which is also stronger because of the additional Meem. In this context of accepting repentance, it implies that Allah even accepts the weak form of repentance التَّوْبِ (Tawb).

Derivative: الطَّوْلِ refers to the ability to reach far from طُول (which means height, length, extending far). ذِي الطَّوْلِ – “The possessor of الطَّوْلِ” is an expression to say that He has far reach, that His power has a great length or that His giving of blessings and bounties is expansive and wide i.e., of far reach, expanse and impact.
- “The forgiver (غَافِرِ) of the sin (الذَّنبِ)” – The use of the فاعِل form implies the present tense i.e., that the action is taking place right now.

Derivative: التَّنَادِ from نَدَى – نِداء – “To call (out)” or “to make a call”. يَوْمَ التَّنَادِ – “The day of التَّنَادِ” refers to the day of judgement in which there will be a lot of calling undertaken, like for example the people of fire calling out to the people of paradise, or the people of Al-A’raf, including both those of Paradise and hell, calling out and the Angels calling to those who were be happy and those who will be miserable etc.
Derivative: الْأَسْبَابَ means causes and means. It refers to all that which is made use of in order to reach something.
Derivative: صَرْحًا – It refers to a tall and loft building or built structure from which you can see everything from its top.
Derivative: تَبَابٍ from تَبَّ. It means ruin, loss and destruction.
- “The path of uprightness or of guidance” – The correct path.
Derivative: مَتَاعٌ – Refers to something that utility is gained from and enjoyment. It is used to imply something that is temporary and short-lived.

Derivative: تَقَلُّبُ can mean to move about i.e., from one place to another. تَقَلُّبُهُمْ فِي الْبِلَادِ “Their تَقَلُّبُ (Taqallub) in the land” refers to their activities which are happening behind the scenes in terms of plotting, planning, meetings and going back and forth to engage in them.

Derivative: دَعْوَةٌ can refer to a claim or invitation i.e., something with substance or something worth being invited to. لَيْسَ لَهُ دَعْوَةٌ “It doesn’t have a دَعْوَةٌ to it" means it has nothing of substance or merit to be called and invited to.

- لَا جَرَمَ is an expression that means: “No doubt about …” or “Truly and for certain …”. It is like making an oath about that which directly follows the expression. It is the introduction to a statement concerning which the person has no doubt concerning its correctness.
Derivative: أُفَوِّضُ from فَوَّضَ – تَفْوِيض and it means to leave or delegate something in someone else’s hands to take care of it for you on your behalf.
Derivative: يُدْحِضُوا from أَدْحَضَ which means to make something fall down. It is used with arguments or ideas with the meaning of to refute i.e., to make them fall down.
Derivative: هَمَّتْ – هَمَّ with بِ is used to mean that someone intends, plans, schemes or has in mind to do something.

Derivative: ذَنبِ (Dhanb) is a sin. It can imply that it is an act that carries embarrassment with it. And it can also refer to something the consequences of which are bad even if it itself is not necessarily bad. However, the ذَنبِ (Dhanb) of the Prophet ﷺ is different from ours.
- Literally means: “The كَلِمَة (word) of your رَبّ (Lord and Master)” in the case where كَلِمَة (word) refers to the verdict or command.
Derivative: تَبَارَكَ means to have immense Barakah (blessing and goodness) and sources of goodness that He has the power to increase as He wishes.
Derivative: صُوَرَ – تَصْوير means to mould, shape, fashion and sculpture something.
Derivative: عَلَقَةٍ refers to a tiny hanging and stuck piece of flesh or form. It is from عَلِقَ which means to hang and stick.
Derivative: يُسْحَبُونَ from سَحَبَ which means to drag along. سُحُبٌ (clouds) is also derived from this meaning as they appear to be dragged across the sky.

Derivative: السَّلَاسِلُ is the plural of سِلْسِلَة which refers to something that is continuous where one part follows on from the other like a series or chain. سَلاسِل refers to a long series of linked chains.

Derivative: تَمْرَحُونَ – From مَرِحَ which means to rejoice, be exuberant and be in high spirits. مَرِحَ is a more intense form of the meaning of فَرِحَ i.e., to expand upon and go even further in terms of heedlessness, enjoying oneself and doing as one likes. Here it is reference to sinful acts and turning away from Allah’s guidance. مَرَحٌ can also refer to pride, contempt and insolence, like in Surah Luqman verse 18: “And do not walk upon the land مَرَحًا”.
Derivative: تَفْرَحُونَ – From فَرِحَ which means to be joyous and celebratory. It refers here to being over joyous or to revel without justification whilst being heedless of Allah.
